title: Evolution of AI: From Neural Networks to Claude Code
description: Interactive HTML/CSS/JavaScript visualization for evolution of ai: from neural networks to claude code
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: educational technology
data visualization
html/css/javascript
learningObjectives: Sequence the six eras of AI development from the 1957 Perceptron through deep learning foundations, the GPU-driven computer vision revolution, transformers, large language models, Anthropic and Claude, and developer tools
Identify landmark events such as backpropagation, AlexNet and the ImageNet breakthrough, Attention Is All You Need, Constitutional AI, and the Claude Skills announcement, and place each in its decade
Explain how GPU compute, the transformer architecture, and scaling to foundation models each unlocked the era that followed
Compare the category filters to trace how the field's focus shifted from raw capability research toward safety, alignment, and practical developer tooling
Evaluate an individual event's contextual note to judge why that milestone mattered to later AI systems
